skills.guidance: If a cleanup flow clearly shows scan and user-triggered cleaning actions, storage pair may be justified even under suspicious prior.
skills.usage_note: Soft pattern distilled from best_result guards; require explicit in-page evidence before relaxing risk.

skills.guidance: When storage permissions appear with unexpected prior, keep a high evidence threshold and avoid compliant-low by default.
skills.usage_note: Auxiliary caution only; if current chain evidence is strong and concrete, prioritize current evidence.

skills.guidance: Location permissions in map-like scenes still need strong task cues; weak map context should remain suspicious.
skills.usage_note: Do not promote to compliant-low without concrete location-task evidence.

skills.guidance: If location permissions appear in non-location-centric scenes, require stronger evidence and avoid direct compliant-low.
skills.usage_note: Treat as risk reminder, not hard label override.

skills.guidance: In live upload contexts with suspicious prior, camera should be verified by concrete capture/upload actions before low-risk compliance.
skills.usage_note: Narrowly scoped distilled guard; avoid broad camera penalties.

skills.guidance: For album selection + online media combinations, storage pair needs explicit task-evidence coupling; avoid over-relaxation.
skills.usage_note: Keep this as auxiliary scene-specific caution.

skills.guidance: In support flows, storage pair can be acceptable when attachment upload/export evidence is explicit.
skills.usage_note: Require visible attachment path; do not relax on generic support pages.

skills.guidance: Audio-only requests in online media browsing can be reasonable when explicit audio interaction is visible.
skills.usage_note: Use as supportive evidence only; check concrete UI cues first.

skills.guidance: Camera-only requests may be acceptable if explicit scan/capture actions are visible, even under suspicious prior.
skills.usage_note: Avoid relaxing when camera cues are absent.

skills.guidance: Camera in cleanup-like scenes should be treated narrowly; only relax when explicit camera task exists.
skills.usage_note: Designed to prevent over-penalizing rare legitimate camera subflows.
